description: Reusing a nonce or IV across two messages under the same key, collapsing the confidentiality and integrity of the cipher.
body: Failure mode: a fixed, zero, or restart-reset nonce/IV is used for more than one message under one key, so for stream/GCM ciphers an attacker can XOR ciphertexts to recover plaintext and may forge authentication tags. Detection signals: the nonce source is constant or derived from low-entropy data; a counter resets on process restart; two ciphertexts share a nonce. Do not let any (key, nonce) pair repeat. Safer fallback: use a persisted increasing counter or sufficiently wide random nonce, rotate the key before nonce exhaustion, or adopt a misuse-resistant mode (AES-GCM-SIV), and test that no nonce repeats across restarts.
